task_completion.description: The layers panel is visible in the image editor interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the layers dialog or panel open on the screen, showing the stack of image layers.

task_completion.description: A new layer has been created above the original background image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layers panel shows at least one new layer in addition to the original background landscape image layer.

task_completion.description: The newly created layer is named 'Overlay Layer'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the layers panel, the text label for the new layer is exactly 'Overlay Layer'.

task_completion.description: The thumbnail of the new layer indicates a white fill.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The small preview thumbnail next to 'Overlay Layer' in the layers panel is solid white.

task_completion.description: The main image canvas is filled with white.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main workspace/canvas area is predominantly white, obscuring the underlying mountain or hiker landscape image.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of the image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Menus, tools, or panels in the image editor were used, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The task was performed in a real image editing application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to a recognized image editor (e.g., GIMP, Photoshop, Photopea) and not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: The white fill was applied to the actual image canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The white area is contained within the image canvas boundaries, not just a white window or shape drawn over the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is a landscape photo (mountain or hiker) containing almost no white pixels. A successful execution will result in the main canvas becoming predominantly white (>=85% white pixels) due to the new layer's fill. The new layer must be named exactly 'Overlay Layer'.
